semanticCore: Snatching, swallowing quickly, catching prey, gulp, or loop
quranicRelevance: Moses' staff swallowing illusions (Talqaf - 'talqafu ma yafikoon', 'talqaf ma sana'oo').

keyDerivations.meaning: To swallow quickly, snatch, devour instantly, gulp, or catch prey

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَأَلْقَىٰ مُوسَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِيَ تَلْقَفُ مَا يَأْفِكُونَ (26:45): Then Moses threw his staff, and suddenly it swallowed up/devoured what they falsified (instant magic debunking).
discardedNoise: Catching baseballs in modern sports fields.
summaryEn: The root represents swift swallowing (Laqf), detailing the miracle of Moses' staff physically swallowing the magical illusions of Pharaoh's magicians.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر اللقف والالتهام السريع للشيء الساقط، واصفاً التهام ثعبان عصى موسى لحبال السحرة وأوهامهم الباطلة (تلقف ما يأفكون).
rawText: تَلَقَّفَهُ مِنْ فَمِهِ 5 He caught it and retained it quickly, [i. e., what he said,] from his mouth. (TA.) لَقِفَهُ 1 He seized it, or took it quickly; (S, M, K;) namely, a thing thrown to him with the hand, or said to him. (M, TA.) See also last sentence of 1 in art. حنك.
